DirectUpload

  • Constructor Details

    • DirectUpload

      public DirectUpload(@Nullable String uploadId, @Nullable Boolean trial, @Nullable DirectUploadStatus status, @Nullable String url, @Nullable Double timeout, @Nullable String corsOrigin, @Nullable DirectUploadResponse pushMediaSettings)

    • DirectUpload

      public DirectUpload()

  • Method Details

    • uploadId

      public Optional<String> uploadId()
      The unique identifier assigned to the media when created. The value must be a valid UUID.

    • trial

      public Optional<Boolean> trial()
      Indicates if the upload was a trial.

    • status

      public Optional<DirectUploadStatus> status()
      Determines the media's status, which can be one of the possible values.

    • url

      public Optional<String> url()
      The url hosts the media file for FastPix, which needs to be download to use further. It supports formats like MP3, MP4, MOV, MKV, or TS, and includes text tracks for subtitles/CC (SRT file/VTT file). While FastPix can handle various audio and video formats and codecs, using standard inputs can help with optimal processing speed.

    • timeout

      public Optional<Double> timeout()
      The duration set for the validity of the upload URL. If the upload isn't completed within this timespan, it's marked as timed out.

    • corsOrigin

      public Optional<String> corsOrigin()
      Upload media directly from a device using the url name or enter "*" to allow all.

    • pushMediaSettings

      public Optional<DirectUploadResponse> pushMediaSettings()

    • builder

      public static DirectUpload.Builder builder()

    • withUploadId

      public DirectUpload withUploadId(@Nullable String uploadId)
      The unique identifier assigned to the media when created. The value must be a valid UUID.

    • withTrial

      public DirectUpload withTrial(@Nullable Boolean trial)
      Indicates if the upload was a trial.

    • withStatus

      public DirectUpload withStatus(@Nullable DirectUploadStatus status)
      Determines the media's status, which can be one of the possible values.

    • withUrl

      public DirectUpload withUrl(@Nullable String url)
      The url hosts the media file for FastPix, which needs to be download to use further. It supports formats like MP3, MP4, MOV, MKV, or TS, and includes text tracks for subtitles/CC (SRT file/VTT file). While FastPix can handle various audio and video formats and codecs, using standard inputs can help with optimal processing speed.

    • withTimeout

      public DirectUpload withTimeout(@Nullable Double timeout)
      The duration set for the validity of the upload URL. If the upload isn't completed within this timespan, it's marked as timed out.

    • withCorsOrigin

      public DirectUpload withCorsOrigin(@Nullable String corsOrigin)
      Upload media directly from a device using the url name or enter "*" to allow all.

    • withPushMediaSettings

      public DirectUpload withPushMediaSettings(@Nullable DirectUploadResponse pushMediaSettings)
